title: Linked origins but distinct roles for extreme length and sequence variation at a tandem repeat in CACNA1C
authors: Song J, Zhao V, Grant RL, Kingsley DM
abstract: Characterizes TRACT, a 30-bp variable number tandem repeat in CACNA1C, dissecting length variation from sequence variation. CACNA1C is a major GWAS locus for schizophrenia and bipolar disorder. Tandem repeat length variation at regulatory loci is an emerging source of disease risk not captured by standard WGS variant callers.
summary: Dissects a 30-bp VNTR in CACNA1C (major psychiatric GWAS locus) into length vs sequence components with distinct roles. Relevant because CACNA1 family (CACNA1A/E/G in our 49-gene set) and the broader point that VNTRs carry disease-relevant regulatory signal missed by SNP-only analyses.
